California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Kirk Alan Pessner 
Paralegal/Administrator 
Political Reports Unit 
March 22, 1988 
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson & Parrinello 
650 California street, Suite 2650 
San Francisco, CA 
94108 
Dear Kirk: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 1-88-103 
This is in reply to your letter dated March 2, 1988, 
confirming our telephone ·conversation concerning sponsored 
committees. 
(2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18419.) 
Your letter accurately reflects our telephone conversation 
and the advice I provided you and, as you requested, I have signed 
and dated your letter. 
It is enclosed. 
Pursuant to our telephone conversation of March 21, 1988, my 
confirmation of the advice reiterated in your letter does not 
constitute formal advice. 
The Commission can issue formal advice 
only to a person whose duties under the Political Reform Act are in 
question or to that person's authorized representative. 
(Section 
18329 (b), copy enclosed.) 
When an authorized represent·ative asks for 
advice on behalf of a person whose duties are in question, the name 
and address of the person must be provided, as well as a specific 
statement that authorization has been made. 
You have not provided the name of the person on whose behalf 
you are seeking advice. 
Therefore, my confirmation of the advice 
reiterated in your letter constitutes informal assistance pursuant to 
Regulation 18329(c).11 
11 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with 
the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. 
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 
18329 (c) (3).) 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • 
(916) 322*5660 


Kirk Alan Pessner 
March 22, 1988 
Page Two 
If you have any questions, please contact me at (916) 
322-5662. 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel /J .

March 2, 1988 
Fair Political Practices Conunission 
Post Office Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95814 
& MUELLER 
Re: 
Regulation. 18419-Sponsored Conunittees 
Dear Jeanne: 
FILE NUMBER 
6290.02 
This letter will memorialize our telephone conversation 
of February 25, 1988 regarding regulation 18419. 
In that con-
versation, I described a situation in which a business entity 
intends to contribute all or nearly all of the contributions to a 
recipient conunittee established pursuant to Government Code 
Section 82013(a) to support a ballot measure on the Kern County 
June 7, 1988 ballot. 
I confirmed that this business entity, in addition to 
making all or nearly all of the contributions to this ballot 
measure committee, has identified itself as the sponsor in the 
name of the ballot measure conunittee (pursuant to Government Code 
84106) and intends to have a responsible officer of the sponsor 
sign the campaign statement in addition to the signature of the 
campaign conunittee treasurer. 
This business entity has made no 
other campaign contributions nor does it intend to make campaign 
contributions other than those to this ballot conunittee. 
You advised us that it appears as though the business 
enti ty is the spom;or of the conuni ttee and is itself not required 
to file as a conunittee under Government Code Section 82013(c) 
since it satisfies all the criteria of Regulation 18419(c). 
If I have misstated any of the information or your 
advice to us, please do not hesitate to contact me. 
If you 
concur with the contents of this letter, please sign and date the
